title: ClickHouse Introduction
content:
ClickHouse Introduction

What is ClickHouse?

ClickHouse is a high-performance, column-oriented SQL database management system for online analytical processing (OLAP). It allows for real-time analytical queries using SQL on large amounts of data.



  • Columnar storage
  • Vectorized query execution
  • Data compression
  • Scalability & Reliability

title: Columnar vs Row-oriented
content:
Columnar vs Row-oriented

Key Differences

Traditional databases (like MySQL, PostgreSQL) store data in rows. ClickHouse stores data in columns, which is much faster for analytical queries.

  • Row-oriented: Great for CRUD and transactional (OLTP) workloads.
  • Column-oriented: Great for aggregation and reading specific columns across many rows (OLAP).
  • IO Efficiency: Only reads the columns needed for the query.

title: ClickHouse Installation
content:
ClickHouse Installation

Quick Start

ClickHouse can be installed on Linux, macOS, and Windows (via Docker or WSL). Here's the easiest way to start:

Download and Run
curl https://clickhouse.com/ | sh
./clickhouse server

Connect via Client
./clickhouse client

title: The MergeTree Engine
content:
The MergeTree Engine

Core Engine

MergeTree is the most powerful and common storage engine in ClickHouse. It supports indexing by primary key, data partitioning, and replication.

Create Table
CREATE TABLE sensors (
    id UInt32,
    timestamp DateTime,
    value Float32
)
ENGINE = MergeTree
ORDER BY (id, timestamp);

title: Inserting Data
content:
Inserting Data

Batch Insertion

ClickHouse is optimized for high-volume batch inserts. Although you can use standard INSERT statements, batching is highly recommended.

Insert Statements
INSERT INTO sensors VALUES (1, now(), 25.5);
INSERT INTO sensors VALUES (2, now(), 26.1);

title: Querying and Aggregation
content:
Querying and Aggregation

Massive Aggregation

ClickHouse excels at aggregating millions of rows in milliseconds. It uses standard SQL but includes many analytical functions.

Aggregating Data
SELECT 
    toStartOfHour(timestamp) AS hour,
    avg(value) AS avg_value
FROM sensors
GROUP BY hour
ORDER BY hour;

title: ClickHouse Data Types
content:
ClickHouse Data Types

Rich Type System

ClickHouse provides specialized types for maximum performance. Common types include:

  • Integers: UInt8 to UInt256, Int8 to Int256
  • Floating Point: Float32, Float64
  • String / FixedString: For text data
  • LowCardinality: Optimized for strings with few unique values
  • Array(T): Dynamic arrays of any type
Using Specialized Types
CREATE TABLE user_tags (
    user_id UInt64,
    tags Array(LowCardinality(String))
)
ENGINE = MergeTree
ORDER BY user_id;

title: Primary Key vs Sorting Key
content:
Primary Key vs Sorting Key

Ordering and Indexing

In ClickHouse, the Sorting Key (ORDER BY) determines how data is stored on disk, while the Primary Key (PRIMARY KEY) determines the sparse index.

  • If no PRIMARY KEY is specified, it defaults to the ORDER BY key.
  • The Sorting Key is critical for query performance and data compression.
  • Choose columns that are frequently used in filters (WHERE clause).
Efficient Indexing
CREATE TABLE events (
    event_date Date,
    event_type String,
    user_id UInt64
)
ENGINE = MergeTree
ORDER BY (event_date, event_type);

title: Data Partitioning
content:
Data Partitioning

Managing Large Datasets

Partitioning allows you to split your data into logical chunks (e.g., by month). This makes data management operations like dropping old data extremely fast.

  • Partitions are independent units of data.
  • Queries can 'prune' partitions to skip irrelevant data entirely.
  • Use partitioning for high-level dimensions like Date.
Partitioning by Month
CREATE TABLE hits (
    url String,
    event_time DateTime
)
ENGINE = MergeTree
PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(event_time)
ORDER BY event_time;

title: TTL (Time To Live)
content:
TTL (Time To Live)

Automatic Data Lifecycle

TTL allows you to automatically delete or move data to slower storage after a certain period of time.

  • Delete: Remove rows older than X days.
  • Column TTL: Clear specific columns while keeping the row.
  • Move: Shift data between disks or S3 buckets.
Auto-delete after 30 days
CREATE TABLE logs (
    msg String,
    ts DateTime
)
ENGINE = MergeTree
ORDER BY ts
TTL ts + INTERVAL 30 DAY;

title: External Dictionaries
content:
External Dictionaries

Fast Lookups from External Sources

Dictionaries allow ClickHouse to join data with external sources (like MySQL, PostgreSQL, or HTTP) with extremely low latency by caching it in memory.

  • Optimized for 'star schema' lookups.
  • Automatically refreshed based on a schedule.
  • Faster than standard JOINs for small to medium lookup tables.
Using dictGet
-- Get category name from dictionary by ID
SELECT 
    product_id,
    dictGet('categories', 'name', product_id) AS category_name
FROM sales;

title: Materialized Views
content:
Materialized Views

Real-time Aggregation

In ClickHouse, Materialized Views act like insertion triggers. They process incoming data and store the result in a target table.

  • Enables sub-second queries on pre-aggregated data.
  • Does not store data itself; it pipes data from source to target.
  • Essential for massive dashbaords.
Aggregating on Ingestion
CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW hourly_sales_mv
TO hourly_sales_table
AS SELECT 
    toStartOfHour(ts) AS hour,
    sum(amount) AS total
FROM raw_sales
GROUP BY hour;

title: Query Profiling (EXPLAIN)
content:
Query Profiling

Understanding Execution

The EXPLAIN statement helps you understand how ClickHouse executes your query and where the bottlenecks might be.

  • EXPLAIN PLAN: Shows the query execution steps.
  • EXPLAIN PIPELINE: Shows how the query is parallelized.
  • EXPLAIN ESTIMATE: Estimates resources (rows/marks) to be read.
Using EXPLAIN
EXPLAIN PLAN header = 1, actions = 1
SELECT count() FROM sensors WHERE id > 100;

title: Data Compression & Codecs
content:
Data Compression & Codecs

Maximizing Disk Efficiency

ClickHouse employs advanced compression algorithms (LZ4, ZSTD) and specialized codecs to reduce storage footprint and increase IO speed.

  • LZ4: Fast compression/decompression (default).
  • ZSTD: Higher compression ratio but more CPU intensive.
  • Delta / DoubleDelta: Optimized for time-series and counters.
  • Gorilla: Specialized for floating point data.
Applying Codecs
CREATE TABLE compressed_data (
    ts DateTime CODEC(DoubleDelta, LZ4),
    price Float64 CODEC(Gorilla, ZSTD)
)
ENGINE = MergeTree
ORDER BY ts;

title: ClickHouse Course Summary
content:
ClickHouse Course Summary

Congratulations! 🚀

You have completed the full ClickHouse course. You now have the knowledge to build and optimize massive analytical systems.

  • Storage: Columnar storage and the MergeTree engine.
  • Optimization: Efficient indexing, partitioning, and compression.
  • Analytics: Materialized views and advanced SQL functions.
  • Lifecycle: Automating data management with TTL.

You are ready to master the world of Big Data with ClickHouse!
